stems: Choose the best sentence for IELTS writing about {topic}.
Which sentence is most natural when citing {reference}?
Pick the sentence with better {topic} accuracy.
Which option sounds most controlled in academic tone for {topic}?
Choose the sentence that uses grammar markers correctly for {topic}.
Which sentence has the best form-meaning-use alignment for {topic}?

patterns.question: Which sentence is strongest if you reference {reference}?
options: {reference} provide useful data for this claim.
{reference} provides useful data for this claim.
{reference} providing useful data for this claim.
{reference} provided useful data always for this claim.
patterns.explanation: Use subject-verb agreement for cited source statements.

patterns.question: Choose the grammatically accurate modal pattern.
options: Students should checks grammar before submit final draft.
Students should check grammar before submitting the final draft.
Students should checked grammar before to submit final draft.
Students should checking grammar before submited final draft.
patterns.explanation: Modal + base verb. 'Before' naturally takes gerund here.

patterns.question: Choose the best sentence for plural/singular agreement in {topic}.
options: There are many reason why {topic} matter.
There are many reasons why {topic} matters.
There is many reasons why {topic} matter.
There are many reasons why {topic} mattering.
patterns.explanation: Plural noun + singular clause subject agreement must be consistent.
